krv.2TI_1_1: 하나님의 뜻으로 말미암아 그리스도 예수 안에 있는 생명의 약속대로 그리스도 예수의 사도 된 바울은
krv.2TI_1_2: 사랑하는 아들 디모데에게 편지하노니 하나님 아버지와 그리스도 예수 우리 주께로부터 은혜와 긍휼과 평강이 네게 있을지어다
krv.2TI_1_3: 나의 밤낮 간구하는 가운데 쉬지 않고 너를 생각하여 청결한 양심으로 조상적부터 섬겨 오는 하나님께 감사하고
krv.2TI_1_4: 네 눈물을 생각하여 너 보기를 원함은 내 기쁨이 가득하게 하려 함이니
krv.2TI_1_5: 이는 네 속에 거짓이 없는 믿음을 생각함이라 이 믿음은 먼저 네 외조모 로이스와 네 어머니 유니게 속에 있더니 네 속에도 있는 줄을 확신하노라
krv.2TI_1_6: 그러므로 내가 나의 안수함으로 네 속에 있는 하나님의 은사를 다시 불일듯 하게 하기 위하여 너로 생각하게 하노니
krv.2TI_1_7: 하나님이 우리에게 주신 것은 두려워하는 마음이 아니요 오직 능력과 사랑과 근신하는 마음이니
krv.2TI_1_8: 그러므로 네가 우리 주의 증거와 또는 주를 위하여 갇힌 자 된 나를 부끄러워 말고 오직 하나님의 능력을 좇아 복음과 함께 고난을 받으라
krv.2TI_1_9: 하나님이 우리를 구원하사 거룩하신 부르심으로 부르심은 우리의 행위대로 하심이 아니요 오직 자기 뜻과 영원한 때 전부터 그리스도 예수 안에서 우리에게 주신 은혜대로 하심이라
krv.2TI_1_10: 이제는 우리 구주 그리스도 예수의 나타나심으로 말미암아 나타났으니 저는 사망을 폐하시고 복음으로써 생명과 썩지 아니할 것을 드러내신지라
krv.2TI_1_11: 내가 이 복음을 위하여 반포자와 사도와 교사로 세우심을 입었노라
krv.2TI_1_12: 이를 인하여 내가 또 이 고난을 받되 부끄러워하지 아니함은 나의 의뢰한 자를 내가 알고 또한 나의 의탁한 것을 그 날까지 저가 능히 지키실 줄을 확신함이라
krv.2TI_1_13: 너는 그리스도 예수 안에 있는 믿음과 사랑으로써 내게 들은 바 바른 말을 본받아 지키고
krv.2TI_1_14: 우리 안에 거하시는 성령으로 말미암아 네게 부탁한 아름다운 것을 지키라
krv.2TI_1_15: 아시아에 있는 모든 사람이 나를 버린 이 일을 네가 아나니 그 중에 부겔로와 허모게네가 있느니라
krv.2TI_1_16: 원컨대 주께서 오네시보로의 집에 긍휼을 베푸시옵소서 저가 나를 자주 유쾌케 하고 나의 사슬에 매인 것을 부끄러워 아니하여
krv.2TI_1_17: 로마에 있을 때에 나를 부지런히 찾아 만났느니라
krv.2TI_1_18: (원컨대 주께서 저로 하여금 그 날에 주의 긍휼을 얻게 하여 주옵소서) 또 저가 에베소에서 얼마큼 나를 섬긴 것을 네가 잘 아느니라
krv.2TI_2_1: 내 아들아 그러므로 네가 그리스도 예수 안에 있는 은혜 속에서 강하고
krv.2TI_2_2: 또 네가 많은 증인 앞에서 내게 들은 바를 충성된 사람들에게 부탁하라 저희가 또 다른 사람들을 가르칠 수 있으리라
krv.2TI_2_3: 네가 그리스도 예수의 좋은 군사로 나와 함께 고난을 받을지니
krv.2TI_2_4: 군사로 다니는 자는 자기 생활에 얽매이는 자가 하나도 없나니 이는 군사로 모집한 자를 기쁘게 하려 함이라
krv.2TI_2_5: 경기하는 자가 법대로 경기하지 아니하면 면류관을 얻지 못할 것이며
krv.2TI_2_6: 수고하는 농부가 곡식을 먼저 받는 것이 마땅하니라
krv.2TI_2_7: 내 말하는 것을 생각하라 주께서 범사에 네게 총명을 주시리라
krv.2TI_2_8: 나의 복음과 같이 다윗의 씨로 죽은 자 가운데서 다시 살으신 예수 그리스도를 기억하라
krv.2TI_2_9: 복음을 인하여 내가 죄인과 같이 매이는 데까지 고난을 받았으나 하나님의 말씀은 매이지 아니하니라
krv.2TI_2_10: 그러므로 내가 택하신 자를 위하여 모든 것을 참음은 저희로도 그리스도 예수 안에 있는 구원을 영원한 영광과 함께 얻게 하려 함이로라
krv.2TI_2_11: 미쁘다 이 말이여, 우리가 주와 함께 죽었으면 또한 함께 살 것이요
krv.2TI_2_12: 참으면 또한 함께 왕 노릇 할 것이요 우리가 주를 부인하면 주도 우리를 부인하실 것이라
krv.2TI_2_13: 우리는 미쁨이 없을지라도 주는 일향 미쁘시니 자기를 부인하실 수 없으시리라
krv.2TI_2_14: 너는 저희로 이 일을 기억하게 하여 말다툼을 하지 말라고 하나님 앞에서 엄히 명하라 이는 유익이 하나도 없고 도리어 듣는 자들을 망하게 함이니라
krv.2TI_2_15: 네가 진리의 말씀을 옳게 분변하여 부끄러울 것이 없는 일꾼으로 인정된 자로 자신을 하나님 앞에 드리기를 힘쓰라
krv.2TI_2_16: 망령되고 헛된 말을 버리라 저희는 경건치 아니함에 점점 나아가나니
krv.2TI_2_17: 저희 말은 독한 창질의 썩어져 감과 같은데 그 중에 후메내오와 빌레도가 있느니라
krv.2TI_2_18: 진리에 관하여는 저희가 그릇되었도다 부활이 이미 지나갔다 하므로 어떤 사람들의 믿음을 무너뜨리느니라
krv.2TI_2_19: 그러나 하나님의 견고한 터는 섰으니 인침이 있어 일렀으되 주께서 자기 백성을 아신다 하며 또 주의 이름을 부르는 자마다 불의에서 떠날지어다 하였느니라
krv.2TI_2_20: 큰 집에는 금과 은의 그릇이 있을 뿐 아니요 나무와 질그릇도 있어 귀히 쓰는 것도 있고 천히 쓰는 것도 있나니
krv.2TI_2_21: 그러므로 누구든지 이런 것에서 자기를 깨끗하게 하면 귀히 쓰는 그릇이 되어 거룩하고 주인의 쓰심에 합당하며 모든 선한 일에 예비함이 되리라
krv.2TI_2_22: 또한 네가 청년의 정욕을 피하고 주를 깨끗한 마음으로 부르는 자들과 함께 의와 믿음과 사랑과 화평을 좇으라
krv.2TI_2_23: 어리석고 무식한 변론을 버리라 이에서 다툼이 나는 줄 앎이라
krv.2TI_2_24: 마땅히 주의 종은 다투지 아니하고 모든 사람을 대하여 온유하라 가르치기를 잘하며 참으며
krv.2TI_2_25: 거역하는 자를 온유함으로 징계할지니 혹 하나님이 저희에게 회개함을 주사 진리를 알게 하실까 하며
krv.2TI_2_26: 저희로 깨어 마귀의 올무에서 벗어나 하나님께 사로잡힌 바 되어 그 뜻을 좇게 하실까 함이라
krv.2TI_3_1: 네가 이것을 알라 말세에 고통하는 때가 이르리니
krv.2TI_3_2: 사람들은 자기를 사랑하며 돈을 사랑하며 자긍하며 교만하며 훼방하며 부모를 거역하며 감사치 아니하며 거룩하지 아니하며
krv.2TI_3_3: 무정하며 원통함을 풀지 아니하며 참소하며 절제하지 못하며 사나우며 선한 것을 좋아 아니하며
krv.2TI_3_4: 배반하여 팔며 조급하며 자고하며 쾌락을 사랑하기를 하나님 사랑하는 것보다 더하며
krv.2TI_3_5: 경건의 모양은 있으나 경건의 능력은 부인하는 자니 이같은 자들에게서 네가 돌아서라
krv.2TI_3_6: 저희 중에 남의 집에 가만히 들어가 어리석은 여자를 유인하는 자들이 있으니 그 여자는 죄를 중히 지고 여러 가지 욕심에 끌린 바 되어
krv.2TI_3_7: 항상 배우나 마침내 진리의 지식에 이를 수 없느니라
krv.2TI_3_8: 얀네와 얌브레가 모세를 대적한 것 같이 저희도 진리를 대적하니 이 사람들은 그 마음이 부패한 자요 믿음에 관하여는 버리운 자들이라
krv.2TI_3_9: 그러나 저희가 더 나가지 못할 것은 저 두 사람의 된 것과 같이 저희 어리석음이 드러날 것임이니라
krv.2TI_3_10: 나의 교훈과 행실과 의향과 믿음과 오래 참음과 사랑과 인내와
krv.2TI_3_11: 핍박과 고난과 또한 안디옥과 이고니온과 루스드라에서 당한 일과 어떠한 핍박 받은 것을 네가 과연 보고 알았거니와 주께서 이 모든 것 가운데서 나를 건지셨느니라
krv.2TI_3_12: 무릇 그리스도 예수 안에서 경건하게 살고자 하는 자는 핍박을 받으리라
krv.2TI_3_13: 악한 사람들과 속이는 자들은 더욱 악하여져서 속이기도 하고 속기도 하나니
krv.2TI_3_14: 그러나 너는 배우고 확신한 일에 거하라 네가 뉘게서 배운 것을 알며
krv.2TI_3_15: 또 네가 어려서부터 성경을 알았나니 성경은 능히 너로 하여금 그리스도 예수 안에 있는 믿음으로 말미암아 구원에 이르는 지혜가 있게 하느니라
krv.2TI_3_16: 모든 성경은 하나님의 감동으로 된 것으로 교훈과 책망과 바르게 함과 의로 교육하기에 유익하니
krv.2TI_3_17: 이는 하나님의 사람으로 온전케 하며 모든 선한 일을 행하기에 온전케 하려 함이니라
krv.2TI_4_1: 하나님 앞과 산 자와 죽은 자를 심판하실 그리스도 예수 앞에서 그의 나타나실 것과 그의 나라를 두고 엄히 명하노니
krv.2TI_4_2: 너는 말씀을 전파하라 때를 얻든지 못 얻든지 항상 힘쓰라 범사에 오래 참음과 가르침으로 경책하며 경계하며 권하라
krv.2TI_4_3: 때가 이르리니 사람이 바른 교훈을 받지 아니하며 귀가 가려워서 자기의 사욕을 좇을 스승을 많이 두고
krv.2TI_4_4: 또 그 귀를 진리에서 돌이켜 허탄한 이야기를 좇으리라
krv.2TI_4_5: 그러나 너는 모든 일에 근신하여 고난을 받으며 전도인의 일을 하며 네 직무를 다하라
krv.2TI_4_6: 관제와 같이 벌써 내가 부음이 되고 나의 떠날 기약이 가까웠도다
krv.2TI_4_7: 내가 선한 싸움을 싸우고 나의 달려갈 길을 마치고 믿음을 지켰으니
krv.2TI_4_8: 이제 후로는 나를 위하여 의의 면류관이 예비되었으므로 주 곧 의로우신 재판장이 그 날에 내게 주실 것이니 내게만 아니라 주의 나타나심을 사모하는 모든 자에게니라
krv.2TI_4_10: 데마는 이 세상을 사랑하여 나를 버리고 데살로니가로 갔고 그레스게는 갈라디아로, 디도는 달마디아로 갔고
krv.2TI_4_11: 누가만 나와 함께 있느니라 네가 올 때에 마가를 데리고 오라 저가 나의 일에 유익하니라
krv.2TI_4_13: 네가 올 때에 내가 드로아 가보의 집에 둔 겉옷을 가지고 오고 또 책은 특별히 가죽 종이에 쓴 것을 가져오라
krv.2TI_4_14: 구리 장색 알렉산더가 내게 해를 많이 보였으매 주께서 그 행한 대로 저에게 갚으시리니
krv.2TI_4_15: 너도 저를 주의하라 저가 우리 말을 심히 대적하였느니라
krv.2TI_4_16: 내가 처음 변명할 때에 나와 함께 한 자가 하나도 없고 다 나를 버렸으나 저희에게 허물을 돌리지 않기를 원하노라
krv.2TI_4_17: 주께서 내 곁에 서서 나를 강건케 하심은 나로 말미암아 전도의 말씀이 온전히 전파되어 이방인으로 듣게 하려 하심이니 내가 사자의 입에서 건지웠느니라
krv.2TI_4_18: 주께서 나를 모든 악한 일에서 건져내시고 또 그의 천국에 들어가도록 구원하시리니 그에게 영광이 세세무궁토록 있을지어다 아멘
krv.2TI_4_19: 브리스가와 아굴라와 및 오네시보로의 집에 문안하라
krv.2TI_4_20: 에라스도는 고린도에 머물렀고 드로비모는 병듦으로 밀레도에 두었노니
krv.2TI_4_21: 겨울 전에 너는 어서 오라 으불로와 부데와 리노와 글라우디아와 모든 형제가 다 네게 문안하느니라
krv.2TI_4_22: 나는 주께서 네 심령에 함께 계시기를 바라노니 은혜가 너희와 함께 있을지어다

kjv.2TI_1_1: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, according to the promise of life which is in Christ Jesus,
kjv.2TI_1_2: To Timothy, my dearly beloved son: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
kjv.2TI_1_3: I thank God, whom I serve from my forefathers with pure conscience, that without ceasing I have remembrance of thee in my prayers night and day;
kjv.2TI_1_4: Greatly desiring to see thee, being mindful of thy tears, that I may be filled with joy;
kjv.2TI_1_5: When I call to remembrance the unfeigned faith that is in thee, which dwelt first in thy grandmother Lois, and thy mother Eunice; and I am persuaded that in thee also.
kjv.2TI_1_6: Wherefore I put thee in remembrance that thou stir up the gift of God, which is in thee by the putting on of my hands.
kjv.2TI_1_7: For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.
kjv.2TI_1_8: Be not thou therefore ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me his prisoner: but be thou partaker of the afflictions of the gospel according to the power of God;
kjv.2TI_1_9: Who hath saved us, and called us with an holy calling, not according to our works, but according to his own purpose and grace, which was given us in Christ Jesus before the world began,
kjv.2TI_1_10: But is now made manifest by the appearing of our Saviour Jesus Christ, who hath abolished death, and hath brought life and immortality to light through the gospel:
kjv.2TI_1_11: Whereunto I am appointed a preacher, and an apostle, and a teacher of the Gentiles.
kjv.2TI_1_12: For the which cause I also suffer these things: nevertheless I am not ashamed: for I know whom I have believed, and am persuaded that he is able to keep that which I have committed unto him against that day.
kjv.2TI_1_13: Hold fast the form of sound words, which thou hast heard of me, in faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.
kjv.2TI_1_14: That good thing which was committed unto thee keep by the Holy Ghost which dwelleth in us.
kjv.2TI_1_15: This thou knowest, that all they which are in Asia be turned away from me; of whom are Phygellus and Hermogenes.
kjv.2TI_1_16: The Lord give mercy unto the house of Onesiphorus; for he oft refreshed me, and was not ashamed of my chain:
kjv.2TI_1_17: But, when he was in Rome, he sought me out very diligently, and found me.
kjv.2TI_1_18: The Lord grant unto him that he may find mercy of the Lord in that day: and in how many things he ministered unto me at Ephesus, thou knowest very well.
kjv.2TI_2_1: Thou therefore, my son, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
kjv.2TI_2_2: And the things that thou hast heard of me among many witnesses, the same commit thou to faithful men, who shall be able to teach others also.
kjv.2TI_2_3: Thou therefore endure hardness, as a good soldier of Jesus Christ.
kjv.2TI_2_4: No man that warreth entangleth himself with the affairs of this life; that he may please him who hath chosen him to be a soldier.
kjv.2TI_2_5: And if a man also strive for masteries, yet is he not crowned, except he strive lawfully.
kjv.2TI_2_6: The husbandman that laboureth must be first partaker of the fruits.
kjv.2TI_2_7: Consider what I say; and the Lord give thee understanding in all things.
kjv.2TI_2_8: Remember that Jesus Christ of the seed of David was raised from the dead according to my gospel:
kjv.2TI_2_9: Wherein I suffer trouble, as an evil doer, even unto bonds; but the word of God is not bound.
kjv.2TI_2_10: Therefore I endure all things for the elect's sakes, that they may also obtain the salvation which is in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.
kjv.2TI_2_11: It is a faithful saying: For if we be dead with him, we shall also live with him:
kjv.2TI_2_12: If we suffer, we shall also reign with him: if we deny him, he also will deny us:
kjv.2TI_2_13: If we believe not, yet he abideth faithful: he cannot deny himself.
kjv.2TI_2_14: Of these things put them in remembrance, charging them before the Lord that they strive not about words to no profit, but to the subverting of the hearers.
kjv.2TI_2_15: Study to shew thyself approved unto God, a workman that needeth not to be ashamed, rightly dividing the word of truth.
kjv.2TI_2_16: But shun profane and vain babblings: for they will increase unto more ungodliness.
kjv.2TI_2_17: And their word will eat as doth a canker: of whom is Hymenaeus and Philetus;
kjv.2TI_2_18: Who concerning the truth have erred, saying that the resurrection is past already; and overthrow the faith of some.
kjv.2TI_2_19: Nevertheless the foundation of God standeth sure, having this seal, The Lord knoweth them that are his. And, let every one that nameth the name of Christ depart from iniquity.
kjv.2TI_2_20: But in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and of silver, but also of wood and of earth; and some to honour, and some to dishonour.
kjv.2TI_2_21: If a man therefore purge himself from these, he shall be a vessel unto honour, sanctified, and meet for the master's use, and prepared unto every good work.
kjv.2TI_2_22: Flee also youthful lusts: but follow righteousness, faith, charity, peace, with them that call on the Lord out of a pure heart.
kjv.2TI_2_23: But foolish and unlearned questions avoid, knowing that they do gender strifes.
kjv.2TI_2_24: And the servant of the Lord must not strive; but be gentle unto all men, apt to teach, patient,
kjv.2TI_2_25: In meekness instructing those that oppose themselves; if God peradventure will give them repentance to the acknowledging of the truth;
kjv.2TI_2_26: And that they may recover themselves out of the snare of the devil, who are taken captive by him at his will.
kjv.2TI_3_1: This know also, that in the last days perilous times shall come.
kjv.2TI_3_2: For men shall be lovers of their own selves, covetous, boasters, proud, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy,
kjv.2TI_3_3: Without natural affection, trucebreakers, false accusers, incontinent, fierce, despisers of those that are good,
kjv.2TI_3_4: Traitors, heady, highminded, lovers of pleasures more than lovers of God;
kjv.2TI_3_5: Having a form of godliness, but denying the power thereof: from such turn away.
kjv.2TI_3_6: For of this sort are they which creep into houses, and lead captive silly women laden with sins, led away with divers lusts,
kjv.2TI_3_7: Ever learning, and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
kjv.2TI_3_8: Now as Jannes and Jambres withstood Moses, so do these also resist the truth: men of corrupt minds, reprobate concerning the faith.
kjv.2TI_3_9: But they shall proceed no further: for their folly shall be manifest unto all men, as their's also was.
kjv.2TI_3_10: But thou hast fully known my doctrine, manner of life, purpose, faith, longsuffering, charity, patience,
kjv.2TI_3_11: Persecutions, afflictions, which came unto me at Antioch, at Iconium, at Lystra; what persecutions I endured: but out of them all the Lord delivered me.
kjv.2TI_3_12: Yea, and all that will live godly in Christ Jesus shall suffer persecution.
kjv.2TI_3_13: But evil men and seducers shall wax worse and worse, deceiving, and being deceived.
kjv.2TI_3_14: But continue thou in the things which thou hast learned and hast been assured of, knowing of whom thou hast learned them;
kjv.2TI_3_15: And that from a child thou hast known the holy scriptures, which are able to make thee wise unto salvation through faith which is in Christ Jesus.
kjv.2TI_3_16: All scripture is given by inspiration of God, and is profitable for doctrine, for reproof, for correction, for instruction in righteousness:
kjv.2TI_3_17: That the man of God may be perfect, thoroughly furnished unto all good works.
kjv.2TI_4_1: I charge thee therefore before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, who shall judge the quick and the dead at his appearing and his kingdom;
kjv.2TI_4_2: Preach the word; be instant in season, out of season; reprove, rebuke, exhort with all long suffering and doctrine.
kjv.2TI_4_3: For the time will come when they will not endure sound doctrine; but after their own lusts shall they heap to themselves teachers, having itching ears;
kjv.2TI_4_4: And they shall turn away their ears from the truth, and shall be turned unto fables.
kjv.2TI_4_5: But watch thou in all things, endure afflictions, do the work of an evangelist, make full proof of thy ministry.
kjv.2TI_4_6: For I am now ready to be offered, and the time of my departure is at hand.
kjv.2TI_4_7: I have fought a good fight, I have finished my course, I have kept the faith:
kjv.2TI_4_8: Henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, shall give me at that day: and not to me only, but unto all them also that love his appearing.
kjv.2TI_4_9: Do thy diligence to come shortly unto me:
kjv.2TI_4_10: For Demas hath forsaken me, having loved this present world, and is departed unto Thessalonica; Crescens to Galatia, Titus unto Dalmatia.
kjv.2TI_4_11: Only Luke is with me. Take Mark, and bring him with thee: for he is profitable to me for the ministry.
kjv.2TI_4_12: And Tychicus have I sent to Ephesus.
kjv.2TI_4_13: The cloke that I left at Troas with Carpus, when thou comest, bring with thee, and the books, but especially the parchments.
kjv.2TI_4_14: Alexander the coppersmith did me much evil: the Lord reward him according to his works:
kjv.2TI_4_15: Of whom be thou ware also; for he hath greatly withstood our words.
kjv.2TI_4_16: At my first answer no man stood with me, but all men forsook me: I pray God that it may not be laid to their charge.
kjv.2TI_4_17: Notwithstanding the Lord stood with me, and strengthened me; that by me the preaching might be fully known, and that all the Gentiles might hear: and I was delivered out of the mouth of the lion.
kjv.2TI_4_18: And the Lord shall deliver me from every evil work, and will preserve me unto his heavenly kingdom: to whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.
kjv.2TI_4_19: Salute Prisca and Aquila, and the household of Onesiphorus.
kjv.2TI_4_20: Erastus abode at Corinth: but Trophimus have I left at Miletum sick.
kjv.2TI_4_21: Do thy diligence to come before winter. Eubulus greeteth thee, and Pudens, and Linus, and Claudia, and all the brethren.
kjv.2TI_4_22: The Lord Jesus Christ be with thy spirit. Grace be with you. Amen.

net.2TI_1_1: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, to further the promise of life in Christ Jesus,
net.2TI_1_2: to Timothy, my dear child. Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord! Thanksgiving and Charge to Timothy
net.2TI_1_3: I am thankful to God, whom I have served with a clear conscience as my ancestors did, when I remember you in my prayers as I do constantly night and day.
net.2TI_1_4: As I remember your tears, I long to see you, so that I may be filled with joy.
net.2TI_1_5: I recall your sincere faith that was alive first in your grandmother Lois and in your mother Eunice, and I am sure is in you.
net.2TI_1_6: Because of this I remind you to rekindle God’s gift that you possess through the laying on of my hands.
net.2TI_1_7: For God did not give us a Spirit of fear but of power and love and self-control.
net.2TI_1_8: So do not be ashamed of the testimony about our Lord or of me, a prisoner for his sake, but by God’s power accept your share of suffering for the gospel.
net.2TI_1_9: He is the one who saved us and called us with a holy calling, not based on our works but on his own purpose and grace, granted to us in Christ Jesus before time began,
net.2TI_1_10: but now made visible through the appearing of our Savior Christ Jesus. He has broken the power of death and brought life and immortality to light through the gospel!
net.2TI_1_11: For this gospel I was appointed a preacher and apostle and teacher.
net.2TI_1_12: Because of this, in fact, I suffer as I do. But I am not ashamed, because I know the one in whom my faith is set and I am convinced that he is able to protect what has been entrusted to me until that day.
net.2TI_1_13: Hold to the standard of sound words that you heard from me and do so with the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
net.2TI_1_14: Protect that good thing entrusted to you, through the Holy Spirit who lives within us.
net.2TI_1_15: You know that everyone in the province of Asia deserted me, including Phygelus and Hermogenes.
net.2TI_1_16: May the Lord grant mercy to the family of Onesiphorus, because he often refreshed me and was not ashamed of my imprisonment.
net.2TI_1_17: But when he arrived in Rome, he eagerly searched for me and found me.
net.2TI_1_18: May the Lord grant him to find mercy from the Lord on that day! And you know very well all the ways he served me in Ephesus.
net.2TI_2_1: So you, my child, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
net.2TI_2_2: And what you heard me say in the presence of many witnesses entrust to faithful people who will be competent to teach others as well.
net.2TI_2_3: Take your share of suffering as a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
net.2TI_2_4: No one in military service gets entangled in matters of everyday life; otherwise he will not please the one who recruited him.
net.2TI_2_5: Also, if anyone competes as an athlete, he will not be crowned as the winner unless he competes according to the rules.
net.2TI_2_6: The farmer who works hard ought to have the first share of the crops.
net.2TI_2_7: Think about what I am saying and the Lord will give you understanding of all this.
net.2TI_2_8: Remember Jesus Christ, raised from the dead, a descendant of David; such is my gospel,
net.2TI_2_9: for which I suffer hardship to the point of imprisonment as a criminal, but God’s message is not imprisoned!
net.2TI_2_10: So I endure all things for the sake of those chosen by God, that they too may obtain salvation in Christ Jesus and its eternal glory.
net.2TI_2_11: This saying is trustworthy: If we died with him, we will also live with him.
net.2TI_2_12: If we endure, we will also reign with him. If we deny him, he will also deny us.
net.2TI_2_13: If we are unfaithful, he remains faithful, since he cannot deny himself. Dealing with False Teachers
net.2TI_2_14: Remind people of these things and solemnly charge them before the Lord not to wrangle over words. This is of no benefit; it just brings ruin on those who listen.
net.2TI_2_15: Make every effort to present yourself before God as a proven worker who does not need to be ashamed, teaching the message of truth accurately.
net.2TI_2_16: But avoid profane chatter, because those occupied with it will stray further and further into ungodliness,
net.2TI_2_17: and their message will spread its infection like gangrene. Hymenaeus and Philetus are in this group.
net.2TI_2_18: They have strayed from the truth by saying that the resurrection has already occurred, and they are undermining some people’s faith.
net.2TI_2_19: However, God’s solid foundation remains standing, bearing this seal: “The Lord knows those who are his,” and “Everyone who confesses the name of the Lord must turn away from evil.”
net.2TI_2_20: Now in a wealthy home there are not only gold and silver vessels, but also ones made of wood and of clay, and some are for honorable use, but others for ignoble use.
net.2TI_2_21: So if someone cleanses himself of such behavior, he will be a vessel for honorable use, set apart, useful for the Master, prepared for every good work.
net.2TI_2_22: But keep away from youthful passions, and pursue righteousness, faithfulness, love, and peace, in company with others who call on the Lord from a pure heart.
net.2TI_2_23: But reject foolish and ignorant controversies, because you know they breed infighting.
net.2TI_2_24: And the Lord’s slave must not engage in heated disputes but be kind toward all, an apt teacher, patient,
net.2TI_2_25: correcting opponents with gentleness. Perhaps God will grant them repentance and then knowledge of the truth
net.2TI_2_26: and they will come to their senses and escape the devil’s trap where they are held captive to do his will.
net.2TI_3_1: But understand this, that in the last days difficult times will come.
net.2TI_3_2: For people will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boastful, arrogant, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, ungrateful, unholy,
net.2TI_3_3: unloving, irreconcilable, slanderers, without self-control, savage, opposed to what is good,
net.2TI_3_4: treacherous, reckless, conceited, loving pleasure rather than loving God.
net.2TI_3_5: They will maintain the outward appearance of religion but will have repudiated its power. So avoid people like these.
net.2TI_3_6: For some of these insinuate themselves into households and captivate weak women who are overwhelmed with sins and led along by various passions.
net.2TI_3_7: Such women are always seeking instruction, yet never able to arrive at a knowledge of the truth.
net.2TI_3_8: And just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so these people—who have warped minds and are disqualified in the faith —also oppose the truth.
net.2TI_3_9: But they will not go much further, for their foolishness will be obvious to everyone, just like it was with Jannes and Jambres. Continue in What You Have Learned
net.2TI_3_10: You, however, have followed my teaching, my way of life, my purpose, my faith, my patience, my love, my endurance,
net.2TI_3_11: as well as the persecutions and sufferings that happened to me in Antioch, in Iconium, and in Lystra. I endured these persecutions and the Lord delivered me from them all.
net.2TI_3_12: Now in fact all who want to live godly lives in Christ Jesus will be persecuted.
net.2TI_3_13: But evil people and charlatans will go from bad to worse, deceiving others and being deceived themselves.
net.2TI_3_14: You, however, must continue in the things you have learned and are confident about. You know who taught you
net.2TI_3_15: and how from infancy you have known the holy writings, which are able to give you wisdom for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.
net.2TI_3_16: Every scripture is inspired by God and useful for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for training in righteousness,
net.2TI_3_17: that the person dedicated to God may be capable and equipped for every good work.
net.2TI_4_1: I solemnly charge you before God and Christ Jesus, who is going to judge the living and the dead, and by his appearing and his kingdom:
net.2TI_4_2: Preach the message, be ready whether it is convenient or not, reprove, rebuke, exhort with complete patience and instruction.
net.2TI_4_3: For there will be a time when people will not tolerate sound teaching. Instead, following their own desires, they will accumulate teachers for themselves, because they have an insatiable curiosity to hear new things.
net.2TI_4_4: And they will turn away from hearing the truth, but on the other hand they will turn aside to myths.
net.2TI_4_5: You, however, be self-controlled in all things, endure hardship, do an evangelist’s work, fulfill your ministry.
net.2TI_4_6: For I am already being poured out as an offering, and the time for me to depart is at hand.
net.2TI_4_7: I have competed well; I have finished the race; I have kept the faith!
net.2TI_4_8: Finally the crown of righteousness is reserved for me. The Lord, the righteous Judge, will award it to me in that day—and not to me only, but also to all who have set their affection on his appearing. Travel Plans and Concluding Greetings
net.2TI_4_9: Make every effort to come to me soon.
net.2TI_4_10: For Demas deserted me, since he loved the present age, and he went to Thessalonica. Crescens went to Galatia and Titus to Dalmatia.
net.2TI_4_11: Only Luke is with me. Get Mark and bring him with you, because he is a great help to me in ministry.
net.2TI_4_12: Now I have sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
net.2TI_4_13: When you come, bring with you the cloak I left in Troas with Carpas and the scrolls, especially the parchments.
net.2TI_4_14: Alexander the coppersmith did me a great deal of harm. The Lord will repay him in keeping with his deeds.
net.2TI_4_15: You be on guard against him too, because he vehemently opposed our words.
net.2TI_4_16: At my first defense no one appeared in my support; instead they all deserted me—may they not be held accountable for it.
net.2TI_4_17: But the Lord stood by me and strengthened me, so that through me the message would be fully proclaimed for all the Gentiles to hear. And so I was delivered from the lion’s mouth!
net.2TI_4_18: The Lord will deliver me from every evil deed and will bring me safely into his heavenly kingdom. To him be glory for ever and ever! Amen.
net.2TI_4_19: Greetings to Prisca and Aquila and the family of Onesiphorus.
net.2TI_4_20: Erastus stayed in Corinth. Trophimus I left ill in Miletus.
net.2TI_4_21: Make every effort to come before winter. Greetings to you from Eubulus, Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers and sisters.
net.2TI_4_22: The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you.
